text: God tested Abraham, calling out to him, and Abraham answered, here I am.
God told him to take Isaac, his only son whom he loved, to the land of Moriah and offer him as a burnt offering on a mountain God would show him.
Abraham rose early, saddled his donkey, took two servants and Isaac, cut wood, and set out for the place God had named.
On the third day Abraham looked up and saw the place from a distance.
He told his servants to stay with the donkey while he and the boy went to worship and would come back.
Abraham laid the wood on Isaac and carried the fire and the knife himself, and the two walked on together.
Isaac asked where the lamb was for the offering, since they had fire and wood.
Abraham answered that God himself would provide the lamb, and they walked on together.
At the place God had shown him, Abraham built the altar, arranged the wood, bound Isaac, and laid him on it.
Abraham reached out and took the knife to slay his son.
The angel of the LORD called to him from heaven, and he answered, here I am.
The angel told him not to harm the boy, for now he knew Abraham feared God, since he had not withheld his only son.
Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in a thicket, and offered it instead of his son.
Abraham named the place The LORD Will Provide, still said today as 'on the mount of the LORD it will be provided.'
The angel of the LORD called to Abraham a second time from heaven.
He said the LORD swore by himself that because Abraham had done this and not withheld his son.
He would surely bless Abraham and multiply his offspring like the stars and the sand, and his offspring would possess their enemies' gates.
All nations of the earth would be blessed through his offspring, because Abraham had obeyed.
Abraham returned to his servants, and they went together to Beersheba, where Abraham stayed.
Abraham was told that Milcah too had borne children to his brother Nahor.
Their firstborn was Uz, then Buz, then Kemuel the father of Aram.
Then Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
Bethuel became the father of Rebekah — Milcah bore these eight to Nahor, Abraham's brother.
Nahor's concubine Reumah also bore him sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
